1.name: Single Card
1.description: The simplest spread, ideal for daily guidance or quick questions

3.name: Three Card Spread
3.description: Classic timeline spread: Past → Present → Future

5.name: Five Card Cross
5.description: In-depth analysis: Situation, Challenge, Past, Future, Advice

tips.text: The tarot deck has 78 cards, including 22 Major Arcana representing major life themes and 56 Minor Arcana reflecting everyday life details.
Reversed cards don't necessarily mean "bad"—they may indicate weakened energy, internal expression, or blind spots you need to pay attention to.
Before drawing cards, center yourself with meditation. Focus your attention on your question and let intuition guide your choice.
The Minor Arcana is divided into four suits: Wands (fire/action), Cups (water/emotions), Swords (air/thoughts), and Pentacles (earth/material).
The Three Card Spread is the most commonly used basic spread. It can be read as past-present-future or as situation-action-outcome.
The appearance of Major Arcana cards usually represents important life lessons or major turning points, worth deeper reflection.
Tarot is a mirror that reflects your inner wisdom. The answers have always been within you—the cards simply help you see them.
This system is based on the classic Rider-Waite Tarot, designed by Arthur Edward Waite in 1909.

majorArcana.name: The Fool
majorArcana.upright: New beginnings, unlimited potential, brave pursuits, fearless innocence
majorArcana.reversed: Reckless behavior, lack of planning, foolish risks, irresponsibility
majorArcana.description: A young person at the edge of a cliff, wearing fine clothes, holding a white rose, accompanied by a small dog, gazing at the sky
majorArcana.note: Represents the beginning of the journey, number 0 symbolizes infinite possibilities

majorArcana.name: The Magician
majorArcana.upright: Talent manifested, wishes fulfilled, strong creativity, excellent communication
majorArcana.reversed: Deception and manipulation, wasted talent, indecisiveness, opportunism
majorArcana.description: A man with one hand pointing up and one down, four elemental tools on the table (wand, cup, sword, pentacle), infinity symbol above his head
majorArcana.note: The intermediary between heaven and earth, 'As above, so below'

majorArcana.name: The High Priestess
majorArcana.upright: Subconscious wisdom, keen intuition, hidden depths, inner peace
majorArcana.reversed: Too withdrawn, ignoring intuition, superficiality, secrets exposed
majorArcana.description: A woman seated between two pillars (B and J), holding a scroll, feet on a crescent moon, pomegranate curtain behind
majorArcana.note: B pillar represents Boaz (strength), J pillar represents Jachin (establishment)

majorArcana.name: The Empress
majorArcana.upright: Harvest and plenty, nurturing new life, sensory pleasure, flourishing creativity
majorArcana.reversed: Over-dependence, creative blocks, infertility, materialism
majorArcana.description: A regal woman seated on cushions in a lush garden, wearing a twelve-star crown, holding a scepter, Venus shield at her feet
majorArcana.note: Represents maternal love and the power of nature, associated with Venus

majorArcana.name: The Emperor
majorArcana.upright: Leadership, order and structure, strong will, paternal protection
majorArcana.reversed: Tyranny, excessive control, rigidity and stubbornness, authoritarian
majorArcana.description: A stern man in armor seated on a ram-headed stone throne, holding an ankh scepter and globe, barren mountains behind
majorArcana.note: Represents paternal authority and social order, the complement of The Empress

majorArcana.name: The Hierophant
majorArcana.upright: Tradition and conventions, spiritual guidance, organized religion, institutional learning
majorArcana.reversed: Challenging authority, breaking free from constraints, unorthodox views, excessive conservatism
majorArcana.description: A religious figure seated between two pillars, raising his right hand in blessing, holding a triple cross, two followers kneeling before him
majorArcana.note: Bridge between the divine and humanity, keeper of orthodox wisdom

majorArcana.name: The Lovers
majorArcana.upright: True love, important choices, harmony and trust, values alignment
majorArcana.reversed: Relationship problems, wrong choices, inner conflict, temptation
majorArcana.description: A nude couple stands beneath the archangel Raphael; behind the woman is the Tree of Knowledge with a serpent, behind the man is the Tree of Life
majorArcana.note: Not just romantic love, but the life-changing power of choice

majorArcana.name: The Chariot
majorArcana.upright: Triumph through willpower, overcoming obstacles, self-discipline, moving forward
majorArcana.reversed: Losing direction, out of control, aggression, stalled progress
majorArcana.description: A warrior in a star-canopied chariot, wearing a crescent-moon armored suit, driven by one black and one white sphinx
majorArcana.note: Representing the triumph of will over matter, balancing opposing forces

majorArcana.name: Strength
majorArcana.upright: Inner strength, gentle perseverance, taming impulses, compassionate power
majorArcana.reversed: Self-doubt, weakness, losing control, suppressing emotions
majorArcana.description: A woman gently taming a lion, infinity symbol above her head, surrounded by a garland of flowers
majorArcana.note: True strength is not brute force, but softly conquering hardness

majorArcana.name: The Hermit
majorArcana.upright: Self-reflection, seeking inner truth, spiritual guidance, detachment from worldly concerns
majorArcana.reversed: Loneliness, isolation, stubbornness, refusing help
majorArcana.description: An old man standing atop a snowy mountain, holding a staff in one hand and a lantern containing a six-pointed star in the other
majorArcana.note: The lantern lights the inner path, solitude leads to enlightenment

majorArcana.name: Wheel of Fortune
majorArcana.upright: Fortune turning, new opportunities, karmic cycle, destiny at work
majorArcana.reversed: Bad luck, resisting change, setbacks, karmic debt
majorArcana.description: A great wheel with Hebrew and alchemical symbols, flanked by the sphinx, snake, and Anubis; four creatures in the corners represent the fixed signs
majorArcana.note: The wheel never stops; what goes up must come down

majorArcana.name: Justice
majorArcana.upright: Fair judgment, karmic balance, taking responsibility, truth revealed
majorArcana.reversed: Injustice, evading responsibility, bias and discrimination, losing a legal case
majorArcana.description: A figure seated between two pillars, holding an upright sword in the right hand and balanced scales in the left, wearing a red robe
majorArcana.note: Represents Libra, the sword represents judgment, the scales represent fairness

majorArcana.name: The Hanged Man
majorArcana.upright: Voluntary sacrifice, new perspective, letting go, spiritual growth through patience
majorArcana.reversed: Unnecessary sacrifice, stalling, unwilling to change, feeling stuck
majorArcana.description: A man hanging upside down from a living tree by one foot, free leg bent to form an inverted 4, halo around his head, serene expression
majorArcana.note: Seeing the world from a new angle, growth through surrender

majorArcana.name: Death
majorArcana.upright: Ending and rebirth, deep transformation, letting go of the old, inevitable change
majorArcana.reversed: Resisting change, fear of endings, stagnation, unable to let go
majorArcana.description: A skeleton in black armor on a white horse, holding a black flag with a white rose; a king has fallen, a priest prays, a child and maiden look on
majorArcana.note: Does not signify physical death, but the end of a phase and new beginnings

majorArcana.name: Temperance
majorArcana.upright: Harmony and balance, patience and tolerance, divine intervention, finding the middle way
majorArcana.reversed: Imbalance, impatience, excess, losing direction
majorArcana.description: An angel standing with one foot on land and one in water, pouring water between two cups; an iris blooms nearby, a golden path leads to distant mountains
majorArcana.note: Blending and balancing opposing forces, the golden mean

majorArcana.name: The Devil
majorArcana.upright: Material temptation, obsessive attachment, shadow self, addictive behavior
majorArcana.reversed: Breaking free from bondage, self-awareness, releasing attachments, overcoming addiction
majorArcana.description: A half-goat, half-bat figure seated on a pedestal, an inverted pentagram on its forehead, one chained man and woman below, loose chains they could remove
majorArcana.note: The chains are loose—freedom is in your own hands

majorArcana.name: The Tower
majorArcana.upright: Sudden upheaval, breaking down false structures, painful truth, necessary destruction
majorArcana.reversed: Avoiding disaster, fear of change, postponed upheaval, internal earthquake
majorArcana.description: A tower struck by lightning, its crown blown off, two figures falling, fire and debris raining down; the tower was built on unstable ground
majorArcana.note: What cannot withstand lightning was never truly solid

majorArcana.name: The Star
majorArcana.upright: Hope and faith, spiritual guidance, inner peace, healing and renewal
majorArcana.reversed: Loss of faith, disappointment, disconnection, creative block
majorArcana.description: A nude woman kneeling by a pool, pouring water from two jugs—one onto land, one into water; above her, one large star and seven small ones
majorArcana.note: After the destruction of The Tower comes the hope of The Star

majorArcana.name: The Moon
majorArcana.upright: Illusions and deception, subconscious fears, uncertainty, following intuition through confusion
majorArcana.reversed: Dispelling illusions, overcoming fears, clarity returning, truth surfacing
majorArcana.description: A full moon between two towers, a winding path leading from a pool; a lobster emerges from the water, a dog and wolf howl at the moon
majorArcana.note: The most uncertain card in the Major Arcana, trust your intuition

majorArcana.name: The Sun
majorArcana.upright: Joy and happiness, success and achievement, vitality, optimism and confidence
majorArcana.reversed: Temporary setback, excessive optimism, delayed success, childishness
majorArcana.description: A child riding a white horse under a bright sun, holding a red banner, sunflowers bloom behind a stone wall
majorArcana.note: The most positive card in the Major Arcana, pure joy

majorArcana.name: Judgement
majorArcana.upright: Spiritual awakening, self-evaluation, answering a higher calling, rebirth
majorArcana.reversed: Self-doubt, refusing the call, excessive self-criticism, fear of judgment
majorArcana.description: The archangel Gabriel blows a trumpet in the sky; below, the dead rise from their coffins with open arms, a cross-emblazoned flag unfurls
majorArcana.note: Reviewing past deeds, achieving spiritual rebirth

majorArcana.name: The World
majorArcana.upright: A cycle completed, achieving goals, wholeness and fulfillment, world travel
majorArcana.reversed: Unfinished business, shortcuts taken, incomplete goals, fear of closure
majorArcana.description: A dancing figure within a laurel wreath, holding two wands; in the four corners are the angel, eagle, lion, and bull representing the fixed signs
majorArcana.note: The final card of the Major Arcana, the end of one journey and beginning of the next

wands.name: Ace of Wands
wands.upright: Creative spark, new opportunities, passion ignited, exciting ventures
wands.reversed: Delays, missed inspiration, false starts, lack of energy
wands.description: A hand emerging from a cloud holds a sprouting wand, hills and castle in the background, leaves falling
wands.note: Pure fire element energy, the seed of new ventures or creation

wands.name: Two of Wands
wands.upright: Future planning, bold decisions, expanding horizons, personal power
wands.reversed: Fear of the unknown, poor planning, indecision, limited vision
wands.description: A man stands on a castle holding a wand in one hand and a globe in the other, gazing at the distant sea
wands.note: Planning the next step from a position of achievement

wands.name: Three of Wands
wands.upright: Expanding ventures, overseas opportunities, progress and growth, waiting for results
wands.reversed: Delays in plans, lack of foresight, obstacles to growth, frustration
wands.description: A man stands with his back to the viewer on a cliff, watching ships sail in the distance, three wands planted
wands.note: Plans are in motion, awaiting the return of results

wands.name: Four of Wands
wands.upright: Celebration, happy reunion, stability achieved, joyful community
wands.reversed: Unstable foundation, lack of harmony, transition period, feeling unwelcome
wands.description: Four wands form an archway decorated with garlands, celebratory figures and a castle in the distance
wands.note: Symbol of weddings, housewarmings, graduations, and joyful occasions

wands.name: Five of Wands
wands.upright: Healthy competition, diverse opinions, creative tension, standing your ground
wands.reversed: Avoiding conflict, inner turmoil, finding common ground, ending disputes
wands.description: Five youths brandishing wands in a melee that resembles a contest or game
wands.note: Constructive competition rather than destructive battle

wands.name: Six of Wands
wands.upright: Public recognition, triumph, leadership acknowledged, self-confidence
wands.reversed: Fall from grace, egotism, lack of recognition, private victory
wands.description: A victor on a white horse wears a laurel crown, holds a laureled wand, surrounded by cheering crowds

wands.name: Seven of Wands
wands.upright: Standing your ground, defending beliefs, competitive advantage, courage
wands.reversed: Giving up, overwhelmed, losing position, self-doubt
wands.description: A man stands on high ground, holding a wand to fend off six wands from below, expression resolute
wands.note: In an advantageous position but needing to defend it

wands.name: Eight of Wands
wands.upright: Swift action, rapid progress, travel, messages arriving
wands.description: Eight wands fly diagonally across a clear sky toward the distant ground
wands.note: The only Minor Arcana card without human figures

wands.name: Nine of Wands
wands.upright: Resilience, last stand, defending boundaries, nearly there
wands.reversed: Exhaustion, giving up, paranoia, refusing help
wands.description: An injured man with a bandaged head wearily but firmly grips a wand, eight wands forming a line of defense behind him
wands.note: Having come a long way, one more step remains

wands.name: Ten of Wands
wands.upright: Heavy burdens, taking on too much, approaching completion, responsibility
wands.reversed: Delegating, releasing burdens, breakdown, learning limits
wands.description: A man bent over carries ten heavy wands, trudging toward a town ahead
wands.note: The responsibility and pressure that success brings

wands.name: Page of Wands
wands.upright: New adventures, enthusiasm, free spirit, good news
wands.reversed: Hasty decisions, lack of direction, setbacks, immature behavior
wands.description: A youth in fine clothes curiously examines a sprouting wand in the desert

wands.name: Knight of Wands
wands.upright: Bold action, adventurous spirit, passionate pursuit, high energy
wands.reversed: Recklessness, hasty actions, scattered focus, burnout
wands.description: A knight on a rearing horse brandishes a wand, wearing a yellow tunic with a salamander pattern on his cloak
wands.note: A doer—but watch direction and follow-through

wands.name: Queen of Wands
wands.upright: Confident leadership, warm personality, independent spirit, social charisma
wands.description: The Queen sits on a throne carved with lions and sunflowers, holding a wand and a sunflower, a black cat at her feet
wands.note: Mature fire-sign woman, combining charm and capability

wands.name: King of Wands
wands.upright: Bold leadership, visionary, entrepreneurial spirit, decisive action
wands.description: The King sits on a throne carved with lions and salamanders, holding a sprouting wand, small lizards at his feet

cups.name: Ace of Cups
cups.upright: New love, emotional awakening, spiritual connection, joy overflowing
cups.reversed: Emotional blockage, emptiness, repressed feelings, self-love needed
cups.description: A hand from a cloud holds an overflowing cup with five streams of water, a dove bringing a host into the cup, a lotus pool below
cups.note: Pure water element energy, the seed of new feelings

cups.name: Two of Cups
cups.upright: Mutual attraction, partnership, harmonious connection, balanced relationship
cups.description: A man and woman face each other, each holding a cup in exchange, caduceus and lion head above
cups.note: One of the best love cards, representing genuine union

cups.name: Three of Cups
cups.upright: Joyful gathering, friendship, creative collaboration, celebration
cups.description: Three women raise their cups in celebration, surrounded by abundant fruit and vegetables

cups.name: Four of Cups
cups.reversed: Renewed motivation, accepting opportunities, emerging from withdrawal, gratitude
cups.description: A man sits under a tree with three cups before him; a hand from a cloud offers a fourth, but he ignores it
cups.note: So absorbed in discontent that present opportunities are overlooked

cups.name: Five of Cups
cups.upright: Mourning loss, focusing on negatives, regret, grief
cups.reversed: Moving on, finding hope, acceptance, learning from loss
cups.description: A figure in black looks down at three spilled cups; two upright cups remain behind, a bridge and buildings in the distance
cups.note: Classic grief card, but hope still exists

cups.name: Six of Cups
cups.upright: Happy memories, childhood innocence, reunion, generosity
cups.reversed: Stuck in the past, unrealistic nostalgia, moving forward, growing up
cups.description: A boy offers a cup full of flowers to a girl in a safe courtyard, a guard in the distance
cups.note: Warm childhood scene, innocence and care

cups.name: Seven of Cups
cups.upright: Daydreaming, many options, wishful thinking, imagination
cups.reversed: Making decisions, clarity, grounded choices, reality check
cups.description: A figure faces seven cups in clouds containing a castle, jewels, laurel, dragon, snake, face, and glowing figure
cups.note: Choice overload—discern what you truly want

cups.name: Eight of Cups
cups.upright: Walking away, seeking deeper meaning, emotional courage, spiritual quest
cups.reversed: Fear of change, aimless wandering, staying too long, avoidance
cups.description: A figure in red turns from eight stacked cups, walking with a staff toward distant mountains, sun and moon in the sky
cups.note: Materially satisfied but spiritually empty, seeking higher purpose

cups.name: Nine of Cups
cups.upright: Wishes fulfilled, emotional satisfaction, gratitude, self-contentment
cups.reversed: Greed, dissatisfaction despite abundance, materialism, unfulfilled wishes
cups.description: A portly man sits with arms crossed, a blue curtain behind him displaying nine golden cups in a row
cups.note: Wish card, representing dreams come true

cups.name: Ten of Cups
cups.upright: Happy family, emotional fulfillment, lasting happiness, harmonious home
cups.reversed: Family discord, broken dreams, misaligned values, neglecting loved ones
cups.description: A couple with arms raised gazes at a rainbow and ten cups in the sky, two children playing nearby, a home in the distance
cups.note: The most complete ending of the Cups suit

cups.name: Page of Cups
cups.upright: Creative inspiration, new emotional experience, intuitive message, youthful love
cups.reversed: Emotional immaturity, creative block, escapism, unrealistic expectations
cups.description: A youth in ornate dress holds a cup; a fish inside speaks to him

cups.name: Knight of Cups
cups.upright: Romantic proposal, following the heart, artistic pursuit, charming personality
cups.reversed: Unrealistic expectations, moodiness, disappointment, emotional manipulation
cups.description: A knight on a slow-moving white horse holds a cup, expression gentle, winged helmet
cups.note: Prince Charming archetype—romantic but watch for action

cups.name: Queen of Cups
cups.upright: Emotional intelligence, nurturing nature, intuitive wisdom, compassionate support
cups.description: The Queen sits by the water, gazing at a unique covered cup (the only covered cup), water at her feet
cups.note: Mature water-sign woman, strong intuition and empathy

cups.name: King of Cups
cups.upright: Emotional maturity, diplomatic wisdom, generous spirit, calm under pressure
cups.reversed: Emotional manipulation, moodiness, coldness, suppressed feelings
cups.description: The King sits on a stone throne floating in the sea, holding a cup and a scepter, ships and leaping fish behind
cups.note: Mature water-sign man, balancing reason and emotion

swords.name: Ace of Swords
swords.upright: Mental clarity, new idea, truth revealed, breakthrough thinking
swords.reversed: Confusion, misinformation, mental chaos, clouded judgment
swords.description: A hand from a cloud holds a sword crowned with laurel and palm, a crown at the tip, rugged mountains in the background
swords.note: Pure air element energy, the power of intellect

swords.name: Two of Swords
swords.upright: Difficult choice, avoidance, mental stalemate, needing balance
swords.reversed: Information overload, mental overwhelm, facing fears, making a decision
swords.description: A blindfolded woman sits by the sea, arms crossed holding two swords, crescent moon and rocks behind
swords.note: Avoiding a decision only prolongs the pain

swords.name: Three of Swords
swords.description: Three swords pierce a red heart, storm clouds and rain in the background
swords.note: The most direct heartbreak image in tarot

swords.name: Four of Swords
swords.upright: Needed rest, recovery period, meditation, mental recharge
swords.description: A knight lies on a church sarcophagus, hands clasped, three swords on the wall, one beneath
swords.note: Intentional rest to prepare for the next action

swords.name: Five of Swords
swords.upright: Hollow victory, defeat, conflict, self-interest at others' expense
swords.reversed: Reconciliation, acceptance, moving on, choosing battles wisely
swords.description: A triumphant man gathers swords from the ground as two defeated figures walk away, overcast sky
swords.note: Won the battle but lost more

swords.name: Six of Swords
swords.upright: Moving to calmer waters, leaving difficulty behind, mental shift, travel
swords.reversed: Stuck in a rut, unresolved issues, resistance to change, delayed journey
swords.description: A ferryman carries a woman and child across water, six swords in the boat, calm waters ahead
swords.note: Leaving the storm, sailing toward calm

swords.name: Seven of Swords
swords.upright: Strategy, resourcefulness, working alone, unconventional approach
swords.reversed: Coming clean, conscience, getting caught, honesty
swords.description: A figure stealthily carries five swords away from a camp, two left behind
swords.note: Judge whether it's clever strategy or dishonesty

swords.name: Eight of Swords
swords.upright: Feeling trapped, self-imposed limitations, victim mentality, mental prison
swords.reversed: Breaking free, new perspective, self-empowerment, seeing clearly
swords.description: A blindfolded, bound woman stands in mud surrounded by eight swords, but her feet are free, a castle in the distance
swords.note: The bonds are self-imposed—you can walk out

swords.name: Nine of Swords
swords.reversed: Recovery from anxiety, hope dawning, releasing worry, seeking help
swords.description: A figure sits in bed, face in hands, nine swords hanging in the dark, roses and astrology on the bedding
swords.note: Often psychological suffering rather than actual disaster

swords.name: Ten of Swords
swords.upright: Painful ending, betrayal, hitting rock bottom, forced closure
swords.reversed: Recovery, resilience, worst is over, rebuilding
swords.description: A figure lies face down with ten swords in the back, but golden dawn breaks on the horizon

swords.name: Page of Swords
swords.upright: Intellectual curiosity, new ideas, vigilance, straightforward communication
swords.reversed: Gossip, deception, hurtful words, all talk no action
swords.description: A youth stands on high ground holding a sword, clothes and hair blown by wind, alertly looking around
swords.note: Air-sign youth or news requiring investigation

swords.name: Knight of Swords
swords.upright: Swift action, sharp mind, fearless pursuit, direct communication
swords.reversed: Impulsiveness, aggression, hasty decisions, lack of tact
swords.description: A knight charges on horseback, sword raised, chaotic clouds and bent trees
swords.note: The fastest, most aggressive knight—watch direction

swords.name: Queen of Swords
swords.upright: Clear thinking, independent judgment, direct communication, perceptive wisdom
swords.description: The Queen sits sideways on a high throne, sword in one hand, the other extended, stern expression, clouds and a bird in the sky
swords.note: Mature air-sign woman, wisdom forged through hardship

swords.name: King of Swords
swords.upright: Intellectual authority, clear judgment, ethical leadership, rational decisions
swords.reversed: Abuse of power, manipulation, cold calculations, tyranny
swords.description: The King sits facing forward on his throne, sword in his right hand, left hand hidden, stern expression, trees and birds behind
swords.note: Mature air-sign man, governing by reason

pentacles.name: Ace of Pentacles
pentacles.upright: New financial opportunity, material gain, promising start, abundance
pentacles.reversed: Missed opportunity, financial setback, poor planning, greed
pentacles.description: A hand from a cloud holds a large pentacle; below, a garden of white lilies and red roses leads to distant mountains
pentacles.note: Pure earth element energy, the seed of material opportunity

pentacles.name: Two of Pentacles
pentacles.upright: Juggling priorities, adaptability, time management, flexible approach
pentacles.description: A youth juggles two pentacles with an infinity-shaped ribbon, ships on choppy seas behind

pentacles.name: Three of Pentacles
pentacles.upright: Skilled collaboration, quality work, learning from experts, recognition of talent
pentacles.reversed: Poor teamwork, mediocrity, lack of effort, disorganization
pentacles.description: A stonemason works in a church as two monks or architects discuss plans with him

pentacles.name: Four of Pentacles
pentacles.upright: Financial stability, holding tight, conservative approach, material security
pentacles.reversed: Greed, excessive frugality, losing control, financial anxiety
pentacles.description: A man sits on a stool, pentacle on his head, one in his arms, two under his feet, isolated from the city behind
pentacles.note: Having but not enjoying—a mindset of control and scarcity

pentacles.name: Five of Pentacles
pentacles.upright: Financial difficulty, feeling excluded, health issues, spiritual poverty
pentacles.reversed: Recovery from loss, finding help, improvement, acceptance
pentacles.description: Two ragged figures (one on crutches) pass a church's stained-glass window in the snow
pentacles.note: Help is inside the church, but they haven't entered

pentacles.name: Six of Pentacles
pentacles.upright: Generous giving, receiving help, fair distribution, kindness
pentacles.reversed: Power imbalance, strings attached, debt, selfishness
pentacles.description: A wealthy man holds scales, distributing coins to two kneeling beggars
pentacles.note: Notice whether you're the giver or the receiver

pentacles.name: Seven of Pentacles
pentacles.upright: Evaluating progress, patience, long-term investment, reassessing direction
pentacles.reversed: Impatience, wasted effort, poor returns, wrong direction
pentacles.description: A farmer leans on a hoe, thoughtfully gazing at seven pentacles growing on a vine
pentacles.note: Assessing whether the investment is worth it, whether to adjust

pentacles.name: Eight of Pentacles
pentacles.upright: Skill development, dedicated work, apprenticeship, attention to detail
pentacles.reversed: Lack of focus, perfectionism, rushing, work fatigue
pentacles.description: A craftsman focuses on carving pentacles at a workbench, six finished pieces on the right, a town in the distance

pentacles.name: Nine of Pentacles
pentacles.upright: Financial independence, self-made success, enjoying rewards, elegant living
pentacles.description: An elegant woman stands in a lush vineyard, a falcon on her arm, a snail at her feet, nine pentacles nearby
pentacles.note: Success and abundance earned through independence

pentacles.name: Ten of Pentacles
pentacles.upright: Family wealth, generational legacy, lasting prosperity, long-term security
pentacles.reversed: Family disputes, inheritance issues, financial instability, broken traditions
pentacles.description: Three generations: an elder under an arch stroking a dog, a couple talking, children nearby; ten pentacles arranged like the Tree of Life
pentacles.note: The most complete ending of the Pentacles suit

pentacles.name: Page of Pentacles
pentacles.upright: New skills, financial opportunity, grounded approach, studious nature
pentacles.reversed: Lack of progress, missed chances, unfocused, academic struggles
pentacles.description: A youth stands in a green field, intently gazing at a pentacle in his hands, trees and freshly plowed land in the distance
pentacles.note: Earth-sign youth or practical new opportunity

pentacles.name: Knight of Pentacles
pentacles.upright: Hard-working, reliable, steady progress, patient determination
pentacles.description: A knight sits on a still black horse, gazing at a pentacle, freshly plowed fields in the background
pentacles.note: The steadiest and most reliable—but also the slowest—knight

pentacles.name: Queen of Pentacles
pentacles.upright: Material security, nurturing homemaker, generous and practical, nature connection
pentacles.reversed: Materialism, neglecting spiritual needs, controlling, financial mismanagement
pentacles.description: The Queen sits on a throne decorated with flowers in a garden, a pentacle on her lap, rabbits nearby
pentacles.note: Mature earth-sign woman, strong in material and nurturing

pentacles.name: King of Pentacles
pentacles.upright: Financial success, business acumen, reliable and stable, material abundance
pentacles.reversed: Greed, miserliness, abuse of wealth, stubborn and conservative
pentacles.description: The King in a grapevine robe sits on a bull-carved throne, holding a scepter and pentacle, ripe grapes and castle around
pentacles.note: Mature earth-sign man, symbol of wealth and stability
